questions.prompt: The bowed-out (concave) shape of the production possibilities curve reflects which underlying economic principle?
choices: Constant opportunity cost across all production combinations
Increasing opportunity cost due to specialized resources
Decreasing returns to consumer demand
The law of diminishing marginal utility
questions.correctText: Increasing opportunity cost due to specialized resources

questions.prompt: A point inside the production possibilities curve indicates that the economy is
choices: Producing efficiently at full capacity
Underutilizing its resources
Experiencing economic growth
Operating beyond its long-run potential
questions.correctText: Underutilizing its resources

questions.prompt: Country A can produce 10 cars or 50 computers per hour; Country B can produce 4 cars or 40 computers per hour. Which statement is true?
choices: A has comparative advantage in both goods
B has absolute advantage in cars
A has comparative advantage in cars; B has comparative advantage in computers
B has comparative advantage in cars; A has comparative advantage in computers
questions.correctText: A has comparative advantage in cars; B has comparative advantage in computers

questions.prompt: If the opportunity cost of producing 1 ton of wheat is 4 tons of corn in Country X and 2 tons of corn in Country Y, then mutually beneficial terms of trade for wheat lie between
choices: 1 and 2 tons of corn
2 and 4 tons of corn
4 and 6 tons of corn
Less than 1 ton of corn
questions.correctText: 2 and 4 tons of corn

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a normative economic statement?
choices: The unemployment rate rose to 5.2% last quarter
Higher interest rates reduce business investment
The government should reduce income taxes to promote fairness
Real GDP grew at an annual rate of 3% last year
questions.correctText: The government should reduce income taxes to promote fairness

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT one of the four factors of production?
choices: Land
Labor
Money
Entrepreneurship
questions.correctText: Money

questions.prompt: In the circular flow model, households supply factors of production to firms in the
choices: Product market
Factor market
Financial market
Foreign exchange market
questions.correctText: Factor market

questions.prompt: A central planning authority that allocates all resources and determines what to produce characterizes which economic system?
choices: Pure market economy
Mixed economy
Command economy
Traditional economy
questions.correctText: Command economy

questions.prompt: An outward shift of the entire PPC is most likely caused by
choices: An increase in unemployment
A reduction in consumer spending
An advancement in technology
A decrease in the labor force
questions.correctText: An advancement in technology

questions.prompt: Scarcity exists because
choices: Government regulations restrict production
Human wants exceed available resources
Some countries are poorer than others
Prices are too high for most consumers
questions.correctText: Human wants exceed available resources

questions.prompt: When calculating GDP using the expenditure approach, which of the following is NOT included?
choices: Consumer spending on a new car
Government spending on highway construction
The purchase of a 2010 used home
Net exports of goods and services
questions.correctText: The purchase of a 2010 used home

questions.prompt: If nominal GDP is $20 trillion and the GDP deflator is 125, real GDP equals
choices: $15 trillion
$16 trillion
$22 trillion
$25 trillion
questions.correctText: $16 trillion

questions.prompt: Which of the following would be included in U.S. GDP for 2026?
choices: The value of a homemaker's household work
An illegal cash transaction at a flea market
A new pair of sneakers sold at retail in 2026
A used 2022 car sold by one consumer to another
questions.correctText: A new pair of sneakers sold at retail in 2026

questions.prompt: A worker who has been laid off and is actively searching for a similar job is classified as
choices: Frictionally unemployed
Structurally unemployed
Cyclically unemployed
A discouraged worker
questions.correctText: Cyclically unemployed

questions.prompt: A computer programmer whose skills are made obsolete by new AI technology is best classified as
choices: Frictionally unemployed
Structurally unemployed
Cyclically unemployed
Not in the labor force
questions.correctText: Structurally unemployed

questions.prompt: The natural rate of unemployment consists of
choices: Frictional unemployment only
Frictional plus structural unemployment
Cyclical plus structural unemployment
All three types of unemployment
questions.correctText: Frictional plus structural unemployment

questions.prompt: A discouraged worker who stops searching for a job is counted as
choices: Unemployed, raising the unemployment rate
Employed part-time involuntarily
Out of the labor force, lowering the unemployment rate
Frictionally unemployed
questions.correctText: Out of the labor force, lowering the unemployment rate

questions.prompt: If the nominal interest rate is 7% and the inflation rate is 3%, the approximate real interest rate is
Unexpectedly high inflation tends to benefit
choices: Lenders at the expense of borrowers
Borrowers at the expense of lenders
Workers on fixed contracts
Retirees on fixed incomes
questions.correctText: Borrowers at the expense of lenders

questions.prompt: Menu costs of inflation refer to
choices: Higher restaurant prices during periods of inflation
The costs firms incur to update prices on items and catalogs
The cost of buying menus for new businesses
Increased grocery bills for consumers
questions.correctText: The costs firms incur to update prices on items and catalogs

questions.prompt: Stagflation is characterized by
choices: Low inflation and low unemployment
High inflation and high unemployment
Low inflation and high unemployment
High inflation and low unemployment
questions.correctText: High inflation and high unemployment

questions.prompt: In a CPI market basket, if the cost of the basket rose from $200 in the base year to $260 in the current year, the CPI is
An economy is at the peak of the business cycle when
choices: Real GDP begins to decline after reaching a maximum
Real GDP reaches its lowest point
Inflation falls to zero
Unemployment rises sharply
questions.correctText: Real GDP begins to decline after reaching a maximum

questions.prompt: The aggregate demand curve slopes downward primarily because of which three effects?
choices: Wealth, interest rate, and foreign price effects
Substitution, income, and wealth effects
Real-balance, investment, and tax effects
Money supply, fiscal, and trade effects
questions.correctText: Wealth, interest rate, and foreign price effects

questions.prompt: An increase in consumer confidence will most likely cause
choices: A leftward shift of AD
A rightward shift of AD
A movement along AD to the left
A leftward shift of SRAS
questions.correctText: A rightward shift of AD

questions.prompt: The long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve is
choices: Upward sloping due to sticky wages
Downward sloping due to wealth effects
Vertical at full-employment real GDP
Horizontal at the current price level
questions.correctText: Vertical at full-employment real GDP

questions.prompt: A recessionary gap exists when
choices: Real GDP exceeds full-employment GDP
Real GDP equals full-employment GDP
Real GDP is below full-employment GDP
The price level is rising rapidly
questions.correctText: Real GDP is below full-employment GDP

questions.prompt: If the marginal propensity to consume (MPC) is 0.8, the spending multiplier is
With an MPC of 0.75, a $100 billion increase in government spending will increase real GDP by approximately
choices: $75 billion
$133 billion
$300 billion
$400 billion
questions.correctText: $400 billion

questions.prompt: If the MPC is 0.9, the tax multiplier is approximately
A sudden increase in the price of oil would most likely cause
choices: A rightward shift of AD
A leftward shift of SRAS
A rightward shift of LRAS
A movement up the AD curve
questions.correctText: A leftward shift of SRAS

questions.prompt: Long-run economic growth is best illustrated by
choices: A rightward shift of AD only
A leftward shift of SRAS
A rightward shift of LRAS
A movement along AD to the right
questions.correctText: A rightward shift of LRAS

questions.prompt: An inflationary gap can be closed through
choices: Expansionary fiscal policy
Contractionary fiscal policy
An increase in the money supply
A rightward shift of AD
questions.correctText: Contractionary fiscal policy

questions.prompt: An appreciation of the U.S. dollar against foreign currencies will most likely
choices: Increase AD by raising net exports
Decrease AD by reducing net exports
Shift SRAS to the right
Shift LRAS to the right
questions.correctText: Decrease AD by reducing net exports

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT a function of money?
choices: Medium of exchange
Store of value
Unit of account
Source of intrinsic utility
questions.correctText: Source of intrinsic utility

questions.prompt: Which of the following is included in M1 but not in M2's additional components?
choices: Savings deposits
Small time deposits under $100,000
Money market mutual funds
Currency in circulation
questions.correctText: Currency in circulation

questions.prompt: If the required reserve ratio is 20%, the maximum value of the simple money multiplier is
If the required reserve ratio is 10% and a bank receives a $1,000 cash deposit, the maximum possible increase in the money supply is
In the loanable funds market, the equilibrium price is
choices: The price level
The nominal interest rate
The real interest rate
The required reserve ratio
questions.correctText: The real interest rate

questions.prompt: An increase in the federal budget deficit (financed by borrowing) would most likely
choices: Shift the demand for loanable funds left, lowering real interest rates
Shift the demand for loanable funds right, raising real interest rates
Shift the supply of loanable funds right, lowering real interest rates
Have no effect on the loanable funds market
questions.correctText: Shift the demand for loanable funds right, raising real interest rates

questions.prompt: In the money market, the supply of money is
choices: Upward sloping with respect to the nominal interest rate
Downward sloping with respect to the nominal interest rate
Vertical because it is set by the central bank
Horizontal at the equilibrium interest rate
questions.correctText: Vertical because it is set by the central bank

questions.prompt: An increase in real GDP will cause which shift in the money market?
choices: Money demand shifts right, nominal interest rate rises
Money demand shifts left, nominal interest rate falls
Money supply shifts right, nominal interest rate falls
Money supply shifts left, nominal interest rate rises
questions.correctText: Money demand shifts right, nominal interest rate rises

questions.prompt: If the Federal Reserve wants to increase the money supply through open market operations, it will
choices: Sell government bonds to the public
Buy government bonds from the public
Raise the discount rate
Raise the reserve requirement
questions.correctText: Buy government bonds from the public

questions.prompt: Which Federal Reserve tool involves changing the rate at which the Fed lends directly to commercial banks?
choices: Open market operations
Reserve requirement
Discount rate
Federal funds rate target
questions.correctText: Discount rate

questions.prompt: Quantitative easing refers to the central bank
choices: Raising short-term interest rates aggressively
Buying large quantities of long-term assets to lower long-term rates
Reducing the money supply through bond sales
Eliminating the reserve requirement entirely
questions.correctText: Buying large quantities of long-term assets to lower long-term rates

questions.prompt: A bank has $10,000 in deposits and the required reserve ratio is 15%. The maximum amount it can lend out is
An increase in expected returns on capital investment will, in the loanable funds market,
choices: Shift supply right, lowering the real interest rate
Shift demand right, raising the real interest rate
Shift demand left, lowering the real interest rate
Shift supply left, raising the real interest rate
questions.correctText: Shift demand right, raising the real interest rate

questions.prompt: Expansionary monetary policy can be expected to
choices: Lower interest rates and increase investment
Raise interest rates and reduce investment
Lower interest rates and reduce investment
Have no effect on interest rates
questions.correctText: Lower interest rates and increase investment

questions.prompt: If the economy is in a recession, the most appropriate fiscal policy combination is
choices: Raise taxes and decrease government spending
Lower taxes and increase government spending
Lower taxes and decrease government spending
Raise taxes and increase government spending
questions.correctText: Lower taxes and increase government spending

questions.prompt: The crowding-out effect from expansionary fiscal policy refers to
choices: Government borrowing raising interest rates and reducing private investment
Higher consumer spending replacing government purchases
Imports replacing domestically produced goods
Foreign investment leaving the country during recessions
questions.correctText: Government borrowing raising interest rates and reducing private investment

questions.prompt: Automatic stabilizers include
choices: A discretionary stimulus package
Progressive income taxes and unemployment insurance
An emergency tax cut passed during a recession
Quantitative easing
questions.correctText: Progressive income taxes and unemployment insurance

questions.prompt: In the long run, the Phillips curve is
choices: Downward sloping showing a permanent trade-off
Upward sloping due to supply shocks
Vertical at the natural rate of unemployment
Horizontal at the target inflation rate
questions.correctText: Vertical at the natural rate of unemployment

questions.prompt: An adverse supply shock would cause the short-run Phillips curve to
choices: Shift left (down)
Shift right (up)
Become vertical
Become horizontal
questions.correctText: Shift right (up)

questions.prompt: The dual mandate of the Federal Reserve includes
choices: Price stability and balanced budgets
Maximum employment and price stability
Free trade and full employment
Exchange rate stability and economic growth
questions.correctText: Maximum employment and price stability

questions.prompt: Supply-side economics emphasizes
choices: Stimulating aggregate demand through government spending
Reducing taxes and regulations to incentivize production
Pegging the exchange rate to a foreign currency
Direct price controls to combat inflation
questions.correctText: Reducing taxes and regulations to incentivize production

questions.prompt: A structural budget deficit is one that exists
choices: Only during recessions
Even when the economy is at full employment
Only during expansions
Only when net exports are negative
questions.correctText: Even when the economy is at full employment

questions.prompt: Under rational expectations, an announced and credible expansionary monetary policy in the long run will
choices: Reduce unemployment below the natural rate permanently
Have no effect on real output, only on prices
Cause permanent deflation
Increase real GDP indefinitely
questions.correctText: Have no effect on real output, only on prices

questions.prompt: A government running a deficit and financing it with new money creation rather than borrowing would most likely
choices: Cause deflation
Increase inflationary pressures
Reduce the money supply
Eliminate crowding out and cause deflation
questions.correctText: Increase inflationary pressures

questions.prompt: Which lag refers to the time between recognizing a problem and enacting policy?
choices: Recognition lag
Implementation lag
Impact lag
Adjustment lag
questions.correctText: Implementation lag

questions.prompt: Contractionary monetary policy is most appropriate when
choices: The economy is in a recession
Unemployment is well above the natural rate
The economy is experiencing high inflation
Net exports are negative
questions.correctText: The economy is experiencing high inflation

questions.prompt: The classical view of macroeconomics holds that
choices: Wages and prices are sticky in the short run, requiring government intervention
The economy is self-correcting through flexible prices and wages
Aggregate demand drives long-run output
Monetary policy is more effective than fiscal policy
questions.correctText: The economy is self-correcting through flexible prices and wages

questions.prompt: A higher national debt-to-GDP ratio can lead to
choices: Higher real interest rates due to crowding out
Lower real interest rates due to less government borrowing
An automatic depreciation of the currency
An immediate trade surplus
questions.correctText: Higher real interest rates due to crowding out

questions.prompt: The current account in the balance of payments includes
choices: Net exports of goods and services, net income, and net transfers
Foreign direct investment only
Purchases of foreign financial assets only
Changes in official reserves only
questions.correctText: Net exports of goods and services, net income, and net transfers

questions.prompt: If a country runs a current account deficit, it must
choices: Run a financial/capital account surplus of equal magnitude
Have a budget surplus
Have a fixed exchange rate
Have rising interest rates
questions.correctText: Run a financial/capital account surplus of equal magnitude

questions.prompt: An appreciation of a country's currency will most likely cause
choices: Exports more expensive abroad and imports cheaper at home
Exports cheaper abroad and imports more expensive at home
Both exports and imports to fall
Both exports and imports to rise
questions.correctText: Exports more expensive abroad and imports cheaper at home

questions.prompt: If U.S. real interest rates rise relative to those abroad, the U.S. dollar will most likely
choices: Depreciate as capital flows out
Appreciate as capital flows in
Remain unchanged under a floating system
Cause an immediate trade surplus
questions.correctText: Appreciate as capital flows in

questions.prompt: An expansionary monetary policy in a country with a floating exchange rate will most likely cause that country's currency to
choices: Appreciate due to higher interest rates
Depreciate due to lower interest rates
Remain unchanged because exchange rates are fixed
Depreciate due to falling demand for exports
questions.correctText: Depreciate due to lower interest rates

questions.prompt: An increase in U.S. tastes for Japanese cars will cause, in the foreign exchange market for yen,
choices: A leftward shift in demand for yen, depreciating the yen
A rightward shift in demand for yen, appreciating the yen
A rightward shift in supply of yen, depreciating the yen
No change because preferences don't affect exchange rates
questions.correctText: A rightward shift in demand for yen, appreciating the yen

questions.prompt: Under a fixed exchange rate regime, if the market value of the currency is below the official rate, the central bank must
choices: Buy its own currency using foreign reserves
Sell its own currency to increase supply
Allow the currency to depreciate freely
Raise tariffs on imports
questions.correctText: Buy its own currency using foreign reserves

questions.prompt: A tariff on imported steel will most likely
choices: Lower domestic steel prices and increase imports
Raise domestic steel prices and reduce imports
Have no effect on domestic prices
Improve consumer welfare in the importing country
questions.correctText: Raise domestic steel prices and reduce imports

questions.prompt: If the U.S. experiences higher inflation than its trading partners, in the long run the U.S. dollar will tend to
choices: Appreciate against trading partners' currencies
Depreciate against trading partners' currencies
Remain stable against trading partners' currencies
Become a stronger reserve currency
questions.correctText: Depreciate against trading partners' currencies

questions.prompt: The primary economic argument for free trade is
choices: It eliminates the need for any government
It allows countries to specialize based on comparative advantage and increase total output
It guarantees full employment in all countries
It eliminates trade deficits permanently
questions.correctText: It allows countries to specialize based on comparative advantage and increase total output

questions.prompt: A depreciation of the home currency will, all else equal,
choices: Improve the home country's trade balance over time
Worsen the home country's trade balance over time
Have no effect on the trade balance
Cause an immediate appreciation of foreign currencies in terms of itself only
questions.correctText: Improve the home country's trade balance over time

questions.prompt: If the U.S. government runs a larger budget deficit, in the open economy this is most likely to
choices: Decrease the real interest rate and depreciate the dollar
Increase the real interest rate, appreciate the dollar, and reduce net exports
Have no effect on the exchange rate
Decrease the demand for loanable funds
questions.correctText: Increase the real interest rate, appreciate the dollar, and reduce net exports

questions.prompt: Which combination of monetary and fiscal policy would best fight a recession?
choices: Buy bonds and increase taxes
Sell bonds and decrease government spending
Buy bonds and increase government spending
Raise the reserve requirement and cut taxes
questions.correctText: Buy bonds and increase government spending

questions.prompt: If actual real GDP equals potential real GDP, then the unemployment rate is approximately
choices: Zero
Equal to the cyclical unemployment rate only
Equal to the natural rate of unemployment
Below the natural rate of unemployment
questions.correctText: Equal to the natural rate of unemployment

questions.prompt: The quantity theory of money equation MV = PY implies that, holding V and Y constant, an increase in the money supply M will cause
choices: A proportional decrease in the price level P
A proportional increase in the price level P
An increase in real output Y
A decrease in velocity V
questions.correctText: A proportional increase in the price level P

questions.prompt: A bank's holdings of vault cash and deposits at the Fed in excess of required reserves are called
choices: Required reserves
Excess reserves
Loans outstanding
Demand deposits
questions.correctText: Excess reserves

questions.prompt: Comparing two economies that both produce only cars and bread: which combination definitively indicates economic growth in the long run?
choices: A movement from inside the PPC to a point on the PPC
A movement along the PPC from one efficient point to another
An outward shift of the PPC
A reallocation of production toward bread only
questions.correctText: An outward shift of the PPC

questions.prompt: If the Federal Reserve sells $5 million in government bonds and the required reserve ratio is 25%, the maximum decrease in the money supply is
choices: $5 million
$15 million
$20 million
$25 million
questions.correctText: $20 million
